title: The Concurrent 3280 — Jersey's Last Great Pre-RISC Mini
scope: Option B — full museum report + Jersey Stack-style YouTube video
deliverables: Museum-grade research report (wiki-publishable, exhibit-ready) for InfoAge / VCF Computer Museum
Jersey Stack-style YouTube video (~10-15 min, primary-source heavy)
Wiki entry on VCF wiki (Concurrent 3280 page that does not yet exist)
primary_sources: Nick's personal photos taken inside the 3280 lab over Christmas 1985 (NJ, Perkin-Elmer era)
Facebook archive in 'Concurrent Computer Corp Employees Past and Present' group (https://www.facebook.com/groups/316610718571/) — Mike Martone has shared substantial 3280 content
Nick's first-person memory: was a co-op there, shared an office with Rich Cholumbolo and Rocco Brescia
secondary_sources: Wikipedia: Interdata 7/32 and 8/32, Concurrent Computer Corporation

key_engineers.name: Rich Chirumbolo
key_engineers.next_act: Cypress Semi → garage door opener business

key_engineers.name: Mike Martone
key_engineers.status: alive, active in Facebook alumni group — PRIMARY OUTREACH TARGET

five_beat_outline: 1. Lineage: Interdata 1966 → P-E 1973 → Concurrent spinoff Nov 1985
2. Machine: 3280SP Jan 26 1988, 3280E Nov 29 1988, the last great pre-RISC scalar mini ($1.8M, 76.8 MIPS)
3. People: Yeager, Cholumbolo, Bush, Brescia, Smith, Martone — and Nick the co-op
4. Legacy: Yeager → SGI → R10000 → the silicon revolution. Yeager dies Aug 2017.
5. Personal frame: Nick at InfoAge in 2026, staring at one of the few survivors — write the history before it disappears
next_actions: Reach Mike Martone via Facebook group (PRIMARY)
